title: Examining the Efficacy of Adopting, Implementing and Sustaining Discrete Trial Training Program for Developing Social Communication Skills in a Case with Autism Spectrum Disorder: District Toba Tek Singh of the Punjab Province, Pakistan
author: Hina Hadayat Ali, Hina Fazil
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: This research paper has examined the efficacy of adopting, implementing and sustaining the DTT program for developing social communication skills in a case with an autism spectrum disorder in the district Toba Tek Singh of the Punjab province, Pakistan. The researchers obtained the face and content validity of the self-made research instrument and DTT data recording sheets by five experts. Five steps were performed to carry out the necessities of the study and therefore meet the aim of the study. Visual analysis under Nugent Method was performed to investigate the skill development rate of change across all the treatment phases. It was found that the task objectives were developed at a higher rate of change during phase E.
steps_raw: This research paper has examined the efficacy of adopting, implementing and sustaining the DTT program for developing social communication skills in a case with an autism spectrum disorder in the district Toba Tek Singh of the Punjab province, Pakistan. Five steps were performed to carry out the necessities of the study and therefore meet the aim of the study. Visual analysis under Nugent Method was performed to investigate the skill development rate of change across all the treatment phases. It was found that the task objectives were developed at a higher rate of change during phase E.
keywords: Autism Spectrum Disorder, Discrete Trial Training Level, Trend, Variability, Amount of Change.
